text: **Penelope:** Hey Anna, what are you {reading|読んでいる}?
**Anna:** I'm reading about {fire safety|防火安全}.
**Anna:** Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver named me {Fire Safety Monitor|防火安全モニター}.
**Penelope:** But the only time we have a {fire emergency|火災緊急事態} is when someone burns a piece of {toast|トースト}.
**Anna:** That is true
**Penelope:** But fire safety is very {serious|深刻な/重大な}.
**Anna:** Very serious.
**Anna:** If there is a fire emergency, I have to {help|助ける} everyone out of the {building|建物}.
**Anna:** Well then, if I were you, I'd {learn|学ぶ} as much as possible.
**Anna:** You're right.
**Anna:** And I know just who to {call|電話する/呼ぶ}.
**Anna:** Hmm, I wonder where Anna's going.
**Anna:** Today, we are {reviewing|復習する} {conditionals|条件文} and learning helpful language for {emergencies|緊急事態}.
**Anna:** If Anna {studies|勉強する} hard, she'll be a great fire safety monitor.
**Anna:** That's a {conditional|条件文}.
**Anna:** It uses if
**Anna:** To show that something is true only when something else is true.
**Anna:** Let's find out where Anna is going.
**Anna:** And if you want to learn fire emergency language, keep {watching|見続ける}.
**Anna:** Hi, {firefighter|消防士} Jones.
**Firefighter Jones:** Hey, how are you, Anna?
**Anna:** I'm well, thanks.
**Firefighter Jones:** How are you?
**Anna:** Hi, Firefighter Hatcher.
**Firefighter Hatcher:** Hi, Anna, how are you?
**Anna:** I'm well, thanks.
**Anna:** Welcome to our {firehouse|消防署}.
**Anna:** Thank you.
**Anna:** Come on in.
**Anna:** Thanks.
**Anna:** This is where the {firefighters|消防士} eat.
**Anna:** This is where we {rest|休憩する}.
**Anna:** This is where we {exercise|運動する}.
**Anna:** This is where the fire trucks are {parked|駐車する/停める} and {maintained|整備する/手入れする}.
**Anna:** This place is {beautiful|美しい}.
**Anna:** You know the {firehouse|消防署} is a firefighter's second home
**Anna:** Firefighter Jones, firefighter Hatcher, can you tell me some ways to {prepare|準備する} for a fire emergency?
**Penelope:** Sure, Anna.
**Anna:** There's one really important thing you can do.
**Anna:** It's install a {fire alarm|火災警報器}.
**Anna:** Test it monthly and make sure that
**Anna:** Batteries are fully {charged|充電された}.
**Anna:** Let's talk about those {emergency|緊急の} exits.
**Anna:** Uh in your home, office, and schools, know where your emergency exits are {located|位置している}.
**Anna:** What about {fire extinguishers|消火器}?
**Anna:** Everyone should know how to use a fire extinguisher.
**Anna:** Uh have one handy and practice using it.
**Anna:** What if we need to get out?
**Anna:** We should have an {evacuation|避難} plan and practice getting out safely with fire joys.
**Anna:** If I'm in a building and it's on fire, should I use the elevator?
**Anna:** No, always use the stairs.
**Anna:** What other safety tips should I know?
**Anna:** Stay low, the smoke rises, the air is clear and close to the ground.
**Anna:** Stay low.
**Anna:** Anything else?
**Anna:** If you touch a door and it's hot,
**Anna:** Don't open it.
**Anna:** There might be a fire on the other side.
**Anna:** What do I do if my clothes catch on fire?
**Anna:** Do not {run|走る/走らない}
**Anna:** If you {run|走る/走らない}, the fire will {burn|燃える/燃やす|/bɜːrn/|The wood will burn quickly.} faster.
**Anna:** You must {stop|止まる/停止する}, {drop|落とす/落ちる}, and {roll|転がる/回転する}.
**Anna:** Thanks to
**Anna:** So much.
**Anna:** I've learned a lot and I can't wait to {share|共有する/分かち合う} this {information|情報} with others.
**Anna:** Thanks for coming, Anna.
**Anna:** It's been a {pleasure|喜び/楽しみ} meeting you.
**Penelope:** So Anna, how was the visit to the {fire station|消防署}?
**Anna:** I learned a lot, and {firefighters|消防士} have a very {difficult|難しい/困難な} job.
**Penelope:** Do you smell {smoke|煙}?
**Anna:** No.
**Anna:** So like I was saying, I learned how to {smoke|吸う/燃やす} - Anna, I smell smoke.
**Anna:** If you smell smoke, call the {fire department|消防署}.
**Anna:** If you smell smoke, get out of the building.
**Penelope:** Come on, people, we have a fire {emergency|緊急事態}.
**Anna:** Leave your things and {evacuate|避難する} calmly and quickly.
**Anna:** Very good.
**Penelope:** We have a fire emergency.
**Anna:** No.
**Anna:** Take the stairs.
**Anna:** Let's get out.
**Anna:** Let's get out safely.
**Anna:** We got out in less than six minutes.
**Anna:** Good job, people
**Anna:** Wait, where's Pete?
**Penelope:** I know where he is.
**Penelope:** I'll go get him.
**Anna:** No, never go back into a {burning|燃えている} building.
**Anna:** The fire department's coming.
**Anna:** Stay {calm|落ち着く/冷静に|/kɑːm/|Calm down and take a deep breath.}, Pete!
**Anna:** {Help|助け/援助} is on the way.
